exemplar.title: Does the time needed for an alpine skier to slide down the hill increase when the path connecting the fixed initial and final points becomes longer?

detailedExemplarData.title: Does the time needed for an alpine skier to slide down the hill increase when the path connecting the fixed initial and final points becomes longer?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, focused and maintained throughout the investigation
Topic is communicated accurately with clear purpose and strong signposting
Assumptions are articulated up front, defining a well-scoped physical model
weaknesses: Methodology description lacks explicit citation of numerical algorithms and uncertainty analysis
Opening anecdote is engaging but not explicitly tied into the research context until later
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Focus and Method is handled at a very high level, with precise statement of purpose and a coherent sequence of mathematical steps. Headings and signposts keep the research question central. The only shortfall lies in deeper justification of the chosen numerical methods and tighter linkage of the introductory narrative, placing this work solidly in the 5-6 band but slightly under the flawless standard for a perfect 6.

strengths: Advanced calculus and mechanics concepts (elliptic integrals, separable ODEs) are applied correctly and beyond syllabus level
Terminology such as 'elliptic integral of the second kind' and vector components is used consistently and accurately
weaknesses: Notation alternates between |v| and | vÌ |, which can confuse the reader
Typographical slip (âqâ instead of âgâ) and occasional unit omissions detract from seamless presentation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates very good subject knowledge and consistently relevant application of advanced concepts. Terminology is precise throughout. Minor notation inconsistencies and small typographical errors preclude the top band, placing the work solidly in the 3-4 band with scope for a fully flawless rendering.

strengths: Research is variedâanalytic, series and numerical approaches directly support the research question
Derivations are rigorous and multi-step, with cross-checks for special cases
Visualizations (plots, GeoGebra figures) effectively reinforce analytical conclusions
weaknesses: Critical evaluation of modelling assumptions (e.g., friction, convergence errors) is only briefly addressed
Error bounds for series truncation and numeric integration are not estimated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation shows strong critical thinking: research is clearly relevant, analysis is deep, and conclusions are well supported. However, discussion remains only partially critical, lacking quantitative error analysis and more probing evaluation of assumptions. This positions the work at the top of the 4-6 band for Critical Thinking.

strengths: Report structure is clear and logical, with appropriate headings and numbered equations
Diagrams and tables are labelled and generally enhance readability
weaknesses: Minor spacing and formatting glitches in imported figures
Results table headers and cell formatting could be cleaned up for consistency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is strong, with a clear EE layout and correctly applied formatting conventions. A few small formatting issues (spacing in screenshots, table styling) prevent a flawless execution, placing this work in the mid-band for Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is worded concisely, is mathematically precise and remains the explicit focus from derivation (eq. *) through individual case studies (n = 1,2,3,4) to the limiting argument as nââ.  Each major section re-links results to the core question, demonstrating strong alignment between RQ, method and discussion, consistent with the 5-6 band.

Derivations are rigorous and multi-stepped, culminating in quantitative answers that are cross-checked (straight-line case).  The asymptotic nââ argument further deepens analysis.  However, error estimates for numeric integration and convergence speed of the series are absent, slightly weakening evidential support; hence a solid 7 rather than 9.

Terms such as âelliptic integral of the second kindâ, âseparable differential equationâ, âeccentricityâ, and vector components are introduced correctly and used consistently.  Mathematical notation is generally precise; a few cosmetic inconsistencies (e.g., switching between |v| and |\bar{v}|) prevent perfection, but overall terminology is accurate and sustained, justifying 5.

Diagrams are labelled, equations are typeset, pages appear numbered and sub-headings are consistent.  Minor spacing glitches exist in copied screenshots, yet overall formatting conventions (citation style, integral notation) are correctly applied, warranting the top mark in this band.

The student applies advanced calculus (chain rule, separable ODEs), classical mechanics and special-function theory accurately, moving beyond syllabus level with elliptic integrals and asymptotic reasoning.  Minor slips (typographical âqâ for g, brief units omissions) do not detract from the overall consistently relevant and largely correct application, meriting the upper band though not flawless enough for a 6.

The student articulates a focused investigation that intertwines skiing and calculus, opening with a clearly stated research question and an explicit purpose (to determine whether a longer x-to-y power path yields a longer descent time).  Throughout the essay, headings, signposting and contextual remarks make the mathematical objective and physical context immediately apparent.  Assumptions (e.g., frictionless motion, point-particle model) are stated up front, further clarifying the scope.  Hence the topic is communicated accurately and purposefully, meeting the top-band descriptors.

The essay follows a clear EE structure (title, abstract-like intro, methodology, results, conclusion, bibliography).  Logical flow is enhanced by sectional headings and numbered equations, so the structure actively supports comprehension, satisfying the upper band for Presentation.

The research gathers analytic, series and numerical evidence across a continuum of n values, all directly relevant to the RQ.  Sources are appropriate and synthesis is evident in combining physics and mathematics.  Limitations such as neglected friction are acknowledged but not probed experimentally, so the work is âmostlyâ rather than âconsistentlyâ excellent, fitting an 8 inside the 7-9 band.

The student interprets results (e.g., why times increase near n=0, identifies optimal n range) and comments on modelling assumptions, but evaluation stops short of critiquing technological accuracy or alternative models with friction.  Argument is reasoned and partially critical, aligning with the 4-6 band; mid-band 5 is appropriate.

A coherent methodology is constructed: energy conservation leads to a general integral formula; closed-form, series and numerical techniques are then selected according to tractability.  GeoGebra visualisations and TI-84 integration are used appropriately.  While the range of sources is adequate (textbooks, MathWorld, calculator), citation of specific numerical algorithms and uncertainty checks is limited, so evidence of *effective* selection is good but not exemplary; hence a secure 5.

annotations.comment: The piecewise free-fall and uniform-motion model for $T_\infty$ is innovative, yielding an insightful asymptotic result.
The decomposition of velocity into horizontal and vertical components is handled correctly, showing accurate use of calculus concepts.
The research question is stated clearly and remains the focal point, effectively guiding the mathematical investigation.
The opening anecdote is engaging but could be tightened by explicitly linking personal interest to the research context earlier.
Simplifying assumptions are articulated clearly, signaling a well-defined scope for the physical model.
The justification for neglecting friction and air resistance is brief; consider discussing the potential quantitative impact of these effects.
A typographical slip uses âqâ instead of âgâ for gravitational acceleration; this should be corrected.
Notation for speed alternates between $|\bar v|$ and $|v|$, which may confuse readers; maintain consistent symbol choice.
The application of energy conservation is rigorous, correctly combining kinetic and potential terms.
Definition of parameter $k$ varies between contexts (ellipse vs. $k=2/\sqrt5$); unify and clarify notation throughout.
The binomial series for $(1+x)^{1/2}$ is densely presented; consider breaking the expansion into smaller, labeled steps.
Assuming the path merges to vertical and horizontal segments for large $n$ is plausible but needs a brief justification of the limiting geometry.
When isolating $-dx/dt$ under the square root, note the domain of $f(x)$ must ensure $1 - f(x)\ge0$ for real speed values.
The derivation culminating in the integral formula (*) is clear and well-structured, effectively linking kinematics to calculus.
The closed-form integration for the straight incline is executed correctly, demonstrating solid calculus skills.
The computed time $T_1=0.639\,$s for a 1 m drop seems very small; consider commenting on real-world plausibility.
The figure illustrating the kinematics verification adds substantial clarity by visually confirming the result.
The statement âforces acting on the object are constantâ is ambiguous; clarify that the net component along the incline is constant.
The substitution $f_2(x)=x^2$ with $f_2'(x)=2x$ is applied correctly, laying the groundwork for the more complex integral.
The handling of $|\sin\theta|$ could be streamlined by stating the domain argument up front before deriving Eq. (14).
The transition to the complete elliptic integral $E(k)$ is well explained, linking the problem to advanced special functions.
The infinite series derivation for $E(k)$ demonstrates deep analytical thinking and mastery of powerâseries techniques.
The truncation of the series after four terms lacks an estimate of the omitted remainder; consider including an error bound.
Reflection on results is descriptive but could be expanded to critically evaluate challenges and learning outcomes.
The graphs illustrating $T_n$ vs. $n$ provide clear visual insight into the dependence and reinforce analytical observations.
The results table is informative but headers and cell formatting should be cleaned up, and values cross-checked against text.
The plot relating exponent $n$ to descent time $T_n$ is comprehensive and highlights the non-monotonic behavior effectively.
The informal description of the limit shape as $n\to\infty$ could be strengthened with a rigorous argument on uniform convergence.
The conclusion succinctly restates the derived formula and key finding, reinforcing the research focus effectively.
The conclusion could be enriched by tying findings back to the initial skiing context and practical implications.
Bibliography entries vary in style (e.g., missing italics, inconsistent year placement); apply a consistent citation format.
Online resources lack access dates and URLs could be hyperlinked; include retrieval information for completeness.
